title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not always authorize all requests.
input_row.rationale: The federation apiserver, by default, allows all requests. You should restrict this behavior to only allow the authorization modes that you explicitly use in your environment. For example, if you don't use REST APIs in your environment, it is a good security best practice to switch-off that capability.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --authorization-mode argument exists and is not set to AlwaysAllow .
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --authorization-mode argument to a value other than AlwaysAllow kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment --namespace=federation-system Impact: Only authorized requests will be served.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_authorization_mode_not_always_allow

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the command-line arguments of the API server.
- The command to retrieve the API server configuration can be done using:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command will list the arguments passed to the API server container.
- Specifically, check for the `--authorization-mode` argument in the output.
- **Pass/Fail criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The `--authorization-mode` argument is present and is set to a value other than `AlwaysAllow`.
  - **Fail:** The `--authorization-mode` argument is either not present or is set to `AlwaysAllow`.
